consist of *Bintang-Bintang Kebesaran* (translatable as 'state decorations') and *Pingat-Pingat Kehormatan* (literally 'medals of honour'). Both are awarded by the [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") on the basis of merit, especially on the contributions to the country. The investiture of some of the state decorations also include the conferment of titles, whereby they become part of the awardees' personal names in official correspondence or when mentioned in formal media in the country. The decorations and medals are under the responsibility of *Jabatan Adat Istiadat Negara*, a government department under the [Prime Minister's Office](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_Office_(Brunei) "Prime Minister's Office (Brunei)"), which also oversees Bruneian royal traditions and protocol.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/16/Wedding_Procession_of_Abdul_Mateen_and_Anisha_Rosnah_44.jpg/250px-Wedding_Procession_of_Abdul_Mateen_and_Anisha_Rosnah_44.jpg)](/wiki/File:Wedding_Procession_of_Abdul_Mateen_and_Anisha_Rosnah_44.jpg)

Orders, decorations, and medals on a military officer's [ceremonial dress](/wiki/Ceremonial_dress "Ceremonial dress") in 2024

The **orders, decorations, and medals of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei")** consist of *Bintang-Bintang Kebesaran* (translatable as 'state decorations') and *Pingat-Pingat Kehormatan* (literally 'medals of honour'). Both are awarded by the [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") on the basis of merit, especially on the contributions to the country. The investiture of some of the state decorations also include the conferment of titles, whereby they become part of the awardees' personal names in official correspondence or when mentioned in formal media in the country. The decorations and medals are under the responsibility of *Jabatan Adat Istiadat Negara*, a government department under the [Prime Minister's Office](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_Office_(Brunei) "Prime Minister's Office (Brunei)"), which also oversees Bruneian royal traditions and protocol.

The insignia are displayed in the [Royal Regalia Museum](/wiki/Royal_Regalia_Museum "Royal Regalia Museum") located in the capital.

## Investiture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orders,_decorations,_and_medals_of_Brunei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Investiture")]

[![Lapau building in Bandar Seri Begawan's city centre](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d5/Bandar_Seri_Begawan_Lapau_Diraja_0004.jpg/250px-Bandar_Seri_Begawan_Lapau_Diraja_0004.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bandar_Seri_Begawan_Lapau_Diraja_0004.jpg)

Lapau building in [Bandar Seri Begawan](/wiki/Bandar_Seri_Begawan "Bandar Seri Begawan")'s [city centre](/wiki/Pusat_Bandar,_Brunei "Pusat Bandar, Brunei")

The actual investiture involves awardees being given badges in a ceremony by the Sultan or sometimes the [Crown Prince](/wiki/Crown_Prince_of_Brunei "Crown Prince of Brunei") upon the Sultan's discretion. The ceremony is traditionally held in [Lapau](/wiki/Lapau "Lapau") which is a type of ceremonial hall for royal ceremonies in the [Bruneian culture](/wiki/Bruneian_culture "Bruneian culture"). At present, the state decorations to the third class are always conferred by [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah") in a royal ceremony held annually on the day of the Sultan's birthday on 15 July at Balai Singgahsana Indera Buana, a ceremonial hall within [Istana Nurul Iman](/wiki/Istana_Nurul_Iman "Istana Nurul Iman"), the Sultan's palace. Meanwhile, the state decorations of lower classes as well as medals of honour are conferred by the Sultan or the Crown Prince [Al-Muhtadee Billah](/wiki/Al-Muhtadee_Billah "Al-Muhtadee Billah") in other ceremonies on other days, although commonly held in conjunction with the said birthday celebration. They had been traditionally held at the Lapau building in [Bandar Seri Begawan](/wiki/Bandar_Seri_Begawan "Bandar Seri Begawan")'s [city centre](/wiki/Pusat_Bandar,_Bandar_Seri_Begawan "Pusat Bandar, Bandar Seri Begawan") but in recent years they have been held at the Lapau hall within the palace instead to accommodate the growing number of awardees.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Foreign honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orders,_decorations,_and_medals_of_Brunei&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Foreign honours")]

[British awards](/wiki/Orders,_decorations,_and_medals_of_the_United_Kingdom "Orders, decorations, and medals of the United Kingdom") granted in Brunei generally fall into two main categories. The first category comprises honorary awards given to the Sultan and senior officials, while the second includes ordinary awards granted to British officials and advisors residing in Brunei. Additionally, some native Borneans may have received the [General Service Medal](/wiki/General_Service_Medal_(1918) "General Service Medal (1918)") and [campaign medals](/wiki/British_campaign_medals "British campaign medals"), featuring clasps for "[Brunei](/wiki/Brunei_revolt "Brunei revolt")" and "[Borneo](/wiki/Indonesia%E2%80%93Malaysia_confrontation "Indonesia–Malaysia confrontation")" respectively, in recognition of their services during the period from 1962 to 1966.[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPamm19951421-4)
